\section{OBJECTIVE}
An experienced digital mixed-signal IC design and verification engineer looking to make a bigger impact
 
\section{EDUCATION}

{\sl PhD,} Electrical Computer Engineering, Oregon State University (2014)\\
Concentration: Reliability of ultra low-power digital mixed-signal integrated circuits

{\sl BS,} Electrical Computer Engineering, Oregon State University (2009)\\
Minor: Computer Science
 
 
\section{EXPERIENCE}

   \textbf{Senior Principal Member of Technical Staff:} Currently works at \textit{Analog Devices} (Formally Maxim Integrated) designing, verifying, and modeling digital and mixed-signal IP for a broad range of applications.  (2014-Present)

\begin{itemize}
  \item \textit{Digital and Mixed-Signal IP Projects:} Design, verification, and modeling of Pipeline and SAR ADCs, Hybrid PLLs, power converters, memory controllers, RISCV digital/mixed-signal IP blocks, and process qualification test chips.

 \item \textit{Product Contributions:} RF-to-bits Hybrid-Radio Receivers, Audio DACs, Ultrasound Front-ends, Power Converters, ADCs, Battery Management ICs
 
 \item \textit{Digital Expertise:} Lead team that architected an automated "chip generator" that included AMS+UVM testbench and regmap/model generation, I2C, SPI, OTP, and other digital/mixed-signal IPs that were used in most new designs across Maxim. Currently leading digital IP design and verification projects.

\end{itemize}


\textbf{Design Intern}: Interned at Intel designing I/O circuits for next-generation server processors in 22nm CMOS. (Summer 2012)

\textbf{Instructor}: Taught ECE 473/573 to a mixed class of senior undergrads and graduate students. The class focused on digital design with SystemVerilog, synthesis techniques, and scripting. (Spring 2013)

\textbf{Research Intern}: Spent the summer as a research intern at Fudan University in Shanghai, China. Research focused on low-power and low-energy encryption engine optimization for
integrated RFID systems. (Summer 2011)

\textbf{Research Assistant}: Researched resilient ultra low-power digital circuits and architectures in the VLSI group at OSU (SRC,NSF,DOE,DOD,NSA, and DARPA funded) as well as bio-medical sensor SOCs. (2010 –- 2014)

\textbf{Summer Class Instructor}: Taught class of computer science students over summer term. Material included computer architecture concepts, advanced assembly programming, and digital/CMOS logic. (Summer 2010)

\textbf{Graduate Teaching Assistant:} Taught Computer Architecture / EE classes to undergraduate students. Topics range from digital logic and basic computer architecture to advanced CMOS circuit design. (2009 –- 2011)

%\textbf{Pre-college Outreach Programs Instructor}: Taught web design and other technology-oriented classes to both middle school and high school students through OSU. (2006 –- 2014)

 
\section{TECHNICAL SKILLS}

\textbf{Programming Languages:} JavaScript, TypeScript, Java/Scala, C, C++, PHP, REST, AJAX, JSON, Android SDK, SQL, HTML, Perl, Python, UNIX/Linux Shell Scripting, Verilog, SystemVerilog, Verilog AMS, UVM, VHDL, Skill, \LaTeX, Microsoft Assembly (MASM), AVR Assembly/C/C++/Arduino, TI MSP430 Assembly, SPICE, LabWindows CVI (LabView)

\textbf{Engineering:} Perforce/GIT, Matlab, SPICE/Spectre, Virtuoso, Xilinx FPGA Toolset, Sun Grid Engine, ModelSim, Synopsis Front-End Toolchain (Design Vision, Primetime, etc.), Xcellium/Spectre/Simvision, SoC Encounter (+ CPF/UPF), Altium PCB Design Software, LabView, Focused Ion Bean/Scanning Electron Microscope
